text.title: distribution-channel-research Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for distribution-channel-research as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs hallucinating CPC, CPM, and conversion benchmarks without citing source and date.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to cite source + date for every economic number. Reject answers that fail the citation requirement. CPC/CPM numbers shift 2x+ within 12-24 months.

failure-mode.description: Agents over-recommending SEO and content marketing because training data is saturated with those examples.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly prompt for non-obvious channels (integrations, niche marketplaces, communities, podcasts) and require a justification for why standard channels were or were not selected.

failure-mode.description: Last-click attribution baked into agent reasoning under-crediting dark social, podcast, and community channels.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Feed self-reported attribution data alongside analytics. Last-click alone produces wrong recommendations for top-of-funnel channels that have long conversion cycles.

failure-mode.description: Agent committing to a Phase 1/Phase 2/Phase 3 calendar with specific dates or week counts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Per workspace rules, no time estimates — phases are gated by milestones (e.g., "after 100 paying customers"), not weeks. Phase gates on outcomes prevent premature scaling.
